questions.question: Who is the narrator of ‘The Thief’s Story’?
questions.answer: Hari Singh is the narrator of the story.

questions.question: How old was Hari Singh?
questions.answer: Hari Singh was about fifteen years old.

questions.question: What kind of work did Hari Singh do for Anil?
questions.answer: Hari Singh cooked food and did household work for Anil.

questions.question: Who was Anil?
questions.answer: Anil was a kind, simple, and trusting writer.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh change his name frequently?
questions.answer: He changed his name to escape the police and former employers.

questions.question: Where did Anil keep his money?
questions.answer: Anil kept his money under the mattress.

questions.question: How much money did Hari Singh steal?
questions.answer: Hari Singh stole six hundred rupees.

questions.question: Why was it difficult for Hari Singh to rob Anil?
questions.answer: It was difficult because Anil trusted him completely.

questions.question: Which train did Hari Singh plan to catch?
questions.answer: He planned to catch the Lucknow Express.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh hesitate at the railway station?
questions.answer: He hesitated due to guilt and inner conflict.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh return to Anil?
questions.answer: He returned because he valued education and Anil’s trust.

questions.question: What did Anil give Hari Singh the next morning?
questions.answer: Anil gave Hari Singh a fifty-rupee note.

questions.question: What was special about the fifty-rupee note?
questions.answer: The note was wet from the rain.

questions.question: What does the wet note indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates that Anil knew about the theft.

questions.question: What did Anil promise to teach Hari Singh?
questions.answer: Anil promised to teach him reading and writing.

questions.question: What quality of Anil changed Hari Singh?
questions.answer: Anil’s trust and kindness changed Hari Singh.

questions.question: What type of narration is used in the story?
questions.answer: The story uses first-person narration.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme is trust and transformation.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh admire educated people?
questions.answer: He admired them because they were respected in society.

questions.question: What does Hari Singh want to become in life?
questions.answer: He wants to become a respected and educated man.

questions.question: How does Anil react to the theft?
questions.answer: Anil forgives Hari Singh silently.

questions.question: What human value does the story highlight?
questions.answer: The story highlights forgiveness and trust.

questions.question: What kind of story is ‘The Thief’s Story’?
questions.answer: It is a realistic and moral story.

questions.question: Why can Anil be called a simple man?
questions.answer: Because he lives simply and trusts others easily.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: Trust, kindness, and education can reform a person.
